Chief Complaint:
Dyspnea on Exertion

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
2013-06-22 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft x 4 (LIMA to LAD, SVG to
Diag , SVG to OM, SVG to PDA)

History of Present Illness:
45 y/o male who has some dyspnea on exertion who had abnormal
cardiac CT and MIBI. Underwent cath which revealed three vessel
disease. Then referred for cardiac surgery.

Past Medical History:
Hypertension, HIV+, Hepatitis B, Renal Insufficiency,
Hypophasphatemia, h/o +PPD tx'd w/ INH in past, h/o fractured
nose s/p repair, s/p oral surgery

Social History:
Unemployed. Smokes 1ppd x 20 yrs. Social ETOH. +Recreational
drug use (Marijuana)

Family History:
Father died of MI at age 66. Mother had CABG at age 61.

VS: 72 123/79 5'9" 145#

Neuro: A&O x 3, MAE, non-focal

Pertinent Results:
Echo 08-15: PRE-CPB: There is mild to moderate regional left
ventricular systolic dysfunction. Overall left ventricular
systolic function is mildly depressed. Resting regional wall
motion abnormalities include hypokinesis of the distal
inferolateral, lateral, and anterolateral walls. There are
simple atheroma in the aortic arch and descending thoracic
aorta. The aortic valve leaflets (3) are mildly thickened but
aortic stenosis is not present. No aortic regurgitation is seen.
The mitral valve appears structurally normal with trivial mitral
regurgitation.
CXR 09-08: 1. New very small left apical pneumothorax following
chest tube removal. 2. Slight decrease in right apical
pneumothorax with residual small pneumothorax. 3. Stable
postoperative widening of cardiac contour, likely due to
postoperative pericardial effusion, as this is a change from the
preoperative radiograph.

Brief Hospital Course:
Mr. Mann was a same day admit following pre-operative work-up
done as an outpatient. On 08-15 he was brought to the operating
room where he underwent a coronary artery bypass graft x 4.
Please see operative report for surgical details. He tolerated
the procedure well and was transferred to the CSRU for invasive
monitoring in stable condition. Later on op day his sedation was
weaned, he awoke neurologically intact and was extubated. Later
on op day infectious disease saw pt for his HIV management. His
HIV meds were resumed and they continued to see him throughout
hospital course.  On post-operative day two he was transferred
to the cardiac telemetry floor. Beta blockers and diuretics were
started and he was gently diuresed towards his pre-op weight. On
post-op day three his chest tubes and epicardial pacing wires
were removed. Chest x-ray after chest tube removal revealed
small apical pneumothoraces. Physical therapy followed pt during
entire post-op course for strength and mobility. Repeat chest
x-ray on post-op day four revealed resolvd pneumothoraces. He
was cleared by infectious diseases for discharge, and will be
followed closely by Dr. April as an outpatient. He was discharged
home with VNA services on post-op day 4 with the appropriate
follow-up appointments.

Medications on Admission:
Norvir 100mg qd, Truvada 200-300mg qd, Reyata 300mg qd, Asprin
81mg qd

PMH: Hypertension, HIV+, Hepatitis B, Renal Insufficiency,
Hypophasphatemia, h/o +PPD tx'd w/ INH in past, h/o fractured

Discharge Condition:

Discharge Instructions:
You may take shower. Wash incisions and gently pat dry. Do not
take bath. Do not apply lotions, creams, ointments or powders to
incisions.
Do not drive for 1 month.
Do not lift greater than 10 pounds for 2 months.
If you develop a fever, notice drainage from incisions, or
redness around incisions, please contact office.
[**Name2 (NI) **] to make all follow-up appointments.
